sections.title: Introduction to Conditional
sections.content: The conditional mood is used to express hypothetical, uncertain, or polite actions. It often corresponds to 'would + verb' in English. The conditional is commonly used in requests, suggestions, and hypothetical situations.
examples: Je voudrais un café (I would like a coffee)
Tu devrais étudier (You should study)
Il pourrait venir (He could come)
S'il avait de l'argent, il achèterait une voiture (If he had money, he would buy a car)

sections.title: Formation of Present Conditional
sections.content: The present conditional is formed by taking the future simple stem (which is often the infinitive) and adding the imperfect endings: -ais, -ais, -ait, -ions, -iez, -aient.
examples: Parler: parler- → je parlerais, tu parlerais, il parlerait, nous parlerions, vous parleriez, ils parleraient
Finir: finir- → je finirais, tu finirais, il finirait, nous finirions, vous finiriez, ils finiraient
Vendre: vendr- → je vendrais, tu vendrais, il vendrait, nous vendrions, vous vendriez, ils vendraient

sections.title: Irregular Conditional Stems
sections.content: Verbs that have irregular future stems also have the same irregular stems in the conditional.
examples: Être: ser- → je serais, tu serais, il serait, nous serions, vous seriez, ils seraient
Avoir: aur- → j'aurais, tu aurais, il aurait, nous aurions, vous auriez, ils auraient
Aller: ir- → j'irais, tu irais, il irait, nous irions, vous iriez, ils iraient
Faire: fer- → je ferais, tu ferais, il ferait, nous ferions, vous feriez, ils feraient
Pouvoir: pourr- → je pourrais, tu pourrais, il pourrait, nous pourrions, vous pourriez, ils pourraient
Vouloir: voudr- → je voudrais, tu voudrais, il voudrait, nous voudrions, vous voudriez, ils voudraient
Savoir: saur- → je saurais, tu saurais, il saurait, nous saurions, vous sauriez, ils sauraient
Devoir: devr- → je devrais, tu devrais, il devrait, nous devrions, vous devriez, ils devraient

sections.title: Uses of Present Conditional
sections.content: The present conditional is used for: 1) Polite requests, 2) Hypothetical situations, 3) Advice or suggestions, 4) Expressing uncertainty or doubt.
examples: Je voudrais une glace (I would like an ice cream) - polite request
Tu devrais faire tes devoirs (You should do your homework) - advice
Si j'avais de l'argent, j'achèterais une maison (If I had money, I would buy a house) - hypothetical
Il serait peut-être en retard (He might be late) - uncertainty

sections.title: Past Conditional (Conditionnel Passé)
sections.content: The past conditional is formed with the conditional of avoir or être + the past participle. It expresses hypothetical actions in the past that would have happened under certain conditions.
examples: J'aurais mangé (I would have eaten)
Tu serais parti(e) (You would have left)
Il aurait fini (He would have finished)
Nous aurions pu venir (We would have been able to come)
Si j'avais su, je serais venu (If I had known, I would have come)

sections.title: Conditional in If Clauses
sections.content: Conditional is commonly used in if clauses (si + imperfect, conditional) to express hypothetical situations.
examples: Si j'avais de l'argent, j'achèterais une voiture (If I had money, I would buy a car)
Si tu étudiais plus, tu réussirais (If you studied more, you would succeed)
Si nous partions tôt, nous arriverions à l'heure (If we left early, we would arrive on time)

sections.title: Common Expressions with Conditional
sections.content: Many common polite expressions use the conditional.
examples: Je voudrais... (I would like...)
J'aimerais... (I would like...)
Pourrais-je...? (Could I...?)
Serait-il possible de...? (Would it be possible to...?)
Auriez-vous...? (Would you have...?)
Devrions-nous...? (Should we...?)

sections.title: Practice Exercise
sections.content: Conjugate these verbs in the conditional: parler, finir, vendre, être, avoir, aller, faire, pouvoir. Then write 5 sentences using the conditional in different contexts (polite request, hypothetical, advice).
